components.title: Backup and Recovery Policy
components.description: The backup and recovery policy defines the organisation's requirements for backing up critical systems, applications, and data to ensure business continuity and resilience against ransomware, hardware failure, or disaster. The policy establishes backup frequency, retention periods, offsite storage requirements, encryption of backup data, and the process for testing and validating backups through regular restoration exercises.

control-implementations.description: Control implementations for Backup and Recovery Policy.

implemented-requirements.description: As defined in this policy, backup processes and supporting procedures are developed, implemented and maintained for all systems classified as critical or high business impact. The policy specifies the backup schedule per system tier, backup owner responsibilities, procedures for monitoring backup job success and failure, and the process for escalating failed backups.

As defined in this policy, backups of critical data, applications and settings are performed and retained in accordance with business criticality. The policy defines retention tiers (e.g. 14 daily, 13 weekly, 12 monthly snapshots), mandates offsite or cloud-based backup storage, requires backup encryption (AES-256), and prohibits backups being stored only on the same system as the primary data.

As defined in this policy, backups are synchronised to enable restoration to a common point in time. For systems where data and configuration are stored separately, the policy requires coordinated backup jobs. Restoration testing validates that data and configuration backups can be restored together to a consistent state.

Backup system changes (backup schedule modifications, retention policy changes, backup destination changes) are governed by the organisation's change and configuration management process. This backup and recovery policy is itself reviewed as part of the configuration management plan at least annually.

components.title: Veeam Backup and Replication
components.description: Veeam Backup and Replication is deployed as the primary backup solution for all on-premises virtual machines (VMware vSphere) and physical servers. Performs daily incremental and weekly full backups to local backup storage with replication to an immutable cloud repository (AWS S3 with Object Lock). Backup encryption is enforced via AES-256.
components.purpose: Perform automated, encrypted, tested backups of all production systems.

control-implementations.description: Control implementations for Veeam Backup and Replication.

implemented-requirements.description: Veeam Backup and Replication encrypts all backup files at rest using AES-256 with a per-job encryption key managed by the Veeam key management service. Backup traffic to the cloud repository uses TLS 1.2 in transit.

Veeam replicates all backup data nightly to an AWS S3 bucket in a different region from the primary data centre. AWS S3 Object Lock (WORM) is enabled with a 30-day immutability period to prevent ransomware from deleting offsite backups.

Veeam SureBackup performs automated recovery verification: after each backup job completes, it boots the backed-up VM in an isolated virtual lab environment and runs application-specific heartbeat tests to verify recoverability. Test results are emailed to the IT operations team and logged in Veeam ONE.

components.title: Offsite Backup Storage — AWS S3
components.description: Amazon Web Services S3 (Simple Storage Service) is used as the immutable offsite backup repository for Veeam backups. The S3 bucket is in a different AWS region from the primary workload. S3 Object Lock is enabled in Compliance mode to prevent modification or deletion of backup objects during the retention period.
components.purpose: Provide geographically separated, immutable cloud storage for backup data.

control-implementations.description: Control implementations for Offsite Backup Storage — AWS S3.

implemented-requirements.description: AWS S3 stores all Veeam backup data in a geographically separated region (Sydney primary, Melbourne replica). The S3 bucket is access-controlled via IAM policy — only the Veeam backup server service account can write objects; no account can delete objects within the Object Lock retention window.

Veeam client-side AES-256 encryption is applied before backup data is uploaded to S3. S3 server-side encryption (SSE-S3) is also enabled as a secondary layer. The S3 bucket policy enforces HTTPS-only access (denies HTTP puts and gets).

capabilities.name: Backup and Recovery
capabilities.description: The backup and recovery capability combines policy, automated backup software, and immutable offsite cloud storage to protect organisational data and ensure recoverability. Veeam Backup and Replication performs daily encrypted backups and automated restoration testing; AWS S3 with Object Lock provides immutable offsite storage protected against ransomware; the backup policy governs requirements and review obligations.

incorporates-components.description: Defines backup frequency, retention, encryption, and restoration testing requirements.
Performs daily encrypted backups and automated SureBackup restoration verification.
Provides immutable offsite backup storage with Object Lock ransomware protection.

control-implementations.description: Control implementations for capability: Backup and Recovery. Includes contributions from member components (identified by source-component-uuid prop) and capability-level synthesis where present.
